_fixture_notes: Case 5: Multiple meal_types and meal_formats. Four meal_types (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack) and all three meal_formats (no_reheat, microwave, thermos). Tests classification breadth and whether downstream meal-planning queries handle high-fan-out classifications correctly.
name: Spiced Chickpea & Spinach Hash
description: A versatile chickpea hash that works any time of day. Fixture exercising maximum meal_types and meal_formats breadth.

steps.title: Soften the onion
steps.content: Heat {0001} in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add {0002} and cook for 6-8 minutes until softened and lightly golden.

steps.title: Toast the spices
steps.content: Stir in {0003} and {0004} and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Add chickpeas
steps.content: Add {0005} and toss to coat. Cook for 5 minutes, pressing the chickpeas with a wooden spoon to crisp some of them up.

steps.title: Wilt spinach and finish
steps.content: Add {0006} a handful at a time, stirring until wilted. Squeeze over {0007} and season to taste.

notes: Eats well cold or hot. Holds up in a thermos for lunch, reheats cleanly in the microwave, and can be eaten straight from the fridge as a snack.
